metadata.title: The Revenue Lie Detector Rithanya
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Rithanya Chandran
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Rithanya Chandran — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The project builds a revenue lie detector on a tight one-month engineering timeline. Evaluation demands ground truth, but relying on existing fraud databases is unreliable. The technical foundation is established, using ratios like Days' Sales in Receivables. DSRI specifically catches channel stuffing by exposing revenue inflated ahead of real sales.

artifactLines: The project builds a revenue lie detector on a tight one-month engineering timeline.
Evaluation demands ground truth, but relying on existing fraud databases is unreliable.
The technical foundation is established, using ratios like Days' Sales in Receivables.
DSRI specifically catches channel stuffing by exposing revenue inflated ahead of real sales.

beats.narration_text: The core tension, that evaluating a fraud detector requires ground truth but existing fraud databases are shaky, is what I want to dig into, especially with a one-month timeline and metrics like DSRI catching channel stuffing. Help me: (1) explain how Days' Sales in Receivables actually flags channel stuffing in the numbers; (2) propose how to establish ground truth for fraud without trusting flawed databases; (3) suggest which financial ratios beyond DSRI a one-month build should prioritize first.

props.command: The core tension, that evaluating a fraud detector requires ground truth but existing fraud databases are shaky, is what I want to dig into, especially with a one-month timeline and metrics like DSRI catching channel stuffing. Help me: (1) explain how Days' Sales in Receivables actually flags channel stuffing in the numbers; (2) propose how to establish ground truth for fraud without trusting flawed databases; (3) suggest which financial ratios beyond DSRI a one-month build should prioritize first.
output: Rithanya Chandran is building a machine that catches lies before
Not the criminal kind of lies that land CFOs in handcuffs
Her background is software engineering and data analysis

beats.narration_text: Her timeline is one month. Her background is software engineering and data analysis.
